title: Eurydice to Orpheus - A Picture by Leighton
content:
But give them me, the mouth, the eyes, the brow!
    Let them once more absorb me! One look now
    Will lap me round for ever, not to pass
    Out of its light, though darkness lie beyond:
    Hold me but safe again within the bond
    Of one immortal look! All woe that was,
    Forgotten, and all terror that may be,
    Defied, no past is mine, no future: look at me!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Browning, titled "Eurydice to Orpheus - A Picture by Leighton", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
